_purpose: Reference schema for Kashf demo case-study files. Frontend renders this shape; backend fills it. Every case_*.json file conforms to this structure. Fields marked 'Mistral-generated' or PLACEHOLDER do not exist yet and carry placeholder strings until the backend produces them; a file is 'partial' until then, 'verified' once filled.

_value_kinds.verified: Computed from the real 2023-2025 dataset in prior verification passes. Present as exact numbers.
_value_kinds.placeholder: Mistral narrative / Simulate output not yet produced. Carries a string that starts with 'PLACEHOLDER:' so it is never mistaken for a real value.

title: string — human-readable case title.
status: 'partial' | 'verified' — 'partial' while any Mistral/Simulate placeholder remains; flips to 'verified' only when the backend fills them all.

map.junction_id: string | null — signal junction_id (signal_junctions_reference.csv), or null when no signal is attributable.
map.route_anchors: [[lng, lat], [lng, lat]] — two [lng, lat] points defining the rendered corridor polyline.
map.junction_point: [lng, lat] | null — marker for the co-located junction, or null.
map.severity_color: 'red' | 'amber' | 'green' — from los_f_pct thresholds: red > 4, amber 1-4, green < 1.

triage.rank: int — corridor rank by severity (1 = worst).
triage.los_f_pct: float — % of hours at Level of Service F, count(level_of_service=='F')/N*100.
triage.demand_gap_vph: float — mean(demand_vph) - mean(volume_vph) over all hours.
triage.mean_vc: float — mean vc_ratio over all hours.

diagnosis.type: string — cause class, e.g. SIGNAL_ISSUE, DEMAND_EXCEEDS_ADAPTIVE, WEATHER_INCIDENT, NO_SIGNAL_ATTRIBUTABLE.
diagnosis.evidence: [string] — computed facts supporting the diagnosis (NOT generated).
diagnosis.human_review_required: bool — true when the diagnosis needs a human to confirm.

simulate.method_label: 'Capacity-delay calculation (indicative, not micro-simulated)' — shown verbatim in the UI to signal the model is indicative.

candidates.disruption_score: int — relative disruption to build (higher = more disruptive).

appraisal._purpose: OPTIONAL, additive. Indicative benefit-cost of each tested fix — answers whether it is worth BUILDING infrastructure vs a cheaper operational fix. Present when computed; the frontend renders it when the block exists.
appraisal.method_label: string — shown verbatim to flag the appraisal is indicative, not RTA-costed.

options.capex_aed: int — capital cost ESTIMATE (labelled, not RTA-costed).
options.bcr: float — benefit-cost ratio (benefit = value-of-time x annual vehicle-hours saved, over a 15-yr horizon).
options.annual_benefit_aed: int — monetised annual delay + recovered-demand benefit.
options.annual_vehhours_saved: int — annual vehicle-hours of delay saved.
options.is_infrastructure: bool — true for build options (lane, grade-separation), false for operational.

_optional_blocks.secondary_corridor: Case 2 only. {corridor_id, los_f_pct, demand_gap_vph, mean_vc, pair_distance_km, _comment, route_anchors} for the opposite direction of the same interchange.
_optional_blocks.triage_table: Case 4 (citywide) only. Replaces the single-corridor body with an array of all 18 corridors: [{location_id, area, los_f_pct, demand_gap_vph, mean_vc, severity_color}].
